text:
<start_of_turn>user
Patient: Adult female, 32 years old. Breathing 34 times per minute. No radial pulse palpable. Unresponsive to voice. What is the WHO START triage category?<end_of_turn>
<start_of_turn>model
TRIAGE: IMMEDIATE (Red)

REASONING — WHO START Protocol:
Step 2 — Respiratory Rate: 34/min > 30 threshold → IMMEDIATE
Step 3 — Perfusion: No radial pulse → IMMEDIATE
Step 4 — Mental Status: Unresponsive → IMMEDIATE

Three independent IMMEDIATE triggers confirmed.
This patient needs intervention within minutes.

Actions: High-flow O2, IV access, fluid resuscitation, priority 1 evacuation.

⚠️ All findings must be verified by trained personnel.<end_of_turn>

<start_of_turn>user
Patient: Male, 28 years old. Walking toward me on his own. Cut on left hand, bleeding controlled with his shirt. Alert, talking clearly, breathing normally.<end_of_turn>
<start_of_turn>model
TRIAGE: MINOR (Green)

REASONING — WHO START Protocol:
Step 0 — Walking Wounded: Patient walked to triage → MINOR

Per START, any ambulatory patient is MINOR. Step 0 is applied before individual assessment begins.

Actions: Direct to minor treatment area. Wound care when resources allow. Reassess in 30 minutes — MINOR can deteriorate.

⚠️ All findings must be verified by trained personnel.<end_of_turn>

<start_of_turn>user
Patient: Female, 45 years old. Breathing 18 per minute. Radial pulse present. Capillary refill 1.5 seconds. Cannot follow commands — does not squeeze my hand. Suspected closed femur fracture.<end_of_turn>
<start_of_turn>model
TRIAGE: IMMEDIATE (Red)

REASONING — WHO START Protocol:
Step 2 — RR: 18/min (10-30 range) → PASS
Step 3 — Perfusion: Radial pulse present, CRT 1.5s → PASS
Step 4 — Mental Status: Cannot follow commands → FAIL → IMMEDIATE

Key insight: Even with stable vitals, altered mental status alone triggers IMMEDIATE. A femur fracture can hide 1-2L internal blood loss — this patient may be in compensated shock.

Actions: Traction splint, IV access, neuro assessment, prepare for evacuation.

⚠️ All findings must be verified by trained personnel.<end_of_turn>

<start_of_turn>user
Patient: Male, 38 years old. Burns on chest and arms. Estimated 22 percent TBSA partial thickness. Facial burns present. Voice is hoarse. Breathing 26 per minute. SpO2 reads 91 percent.<end_of_turn>
<start_of_turn>model
TRIAGE: IMMEDIATE (Red) — AIRWAY EMERGENCY

REASONING:
1. INHALATION INJURY SUSPECTED:
   Facial burns + hoarse voice = presumed inhalation injury.
   Airway edema can close the airway within 30-60 minutes.

2. BURN SEVERITY:
   22% TBSA > 20% adult IMMEDIATE threshold (WHO burns).
   Parkland formula: 4 x weight(kg) x 22 = 24h fluid need.

3. HYPOXIA:
   SpO2 91% borderline. CO poisoning gives falsely normal    SpO2 — treat with 100% O2 regardless.

Actions:
1. Secure airway immediately — do not wait
2. High-flow O2 via non-rebreather mask 15L/min
3. Two large-bore IVs, start Parkland fluids
4. Priority 1 evacuation to burns unit

⚠️ All findings must be verified by trained personnel.<end_of_turn>

<start_of_turn>user
Patient: Child, approximately 6 years old. Breathing 28 per minute. Radial pulse present. Alert, following commands, crying loudly. Laceration on forehead, bleeding controlled.<end_of_turn>
<start_of_turn>model
TRIAGE: DELAYED (Yellow)

REASONING — Pediatric JumpSTART Protocol:
Step 2 — RR: 28/min. PEDIATRIC NOTE: Normal RR for age 6 is 15-25/min. Mildly elevated but below 30/min IMMEDIATE threshold.
Step 3 — Perfusion: Radial pulse present → PASS
Step 4 — Mental Status: Following commands → PASS

All steps pass → DELAYED.

PEDIATRIC WARNING: Children compensate well then crash suddenly without warning. Reassess every 10-15 minutes.

Actions: Wound care, reassess frequently.

⚠️ All findings must be verified by trained personnel.<end_of_turn>

<start_of_turn>user
Patient: Male, 55 years old. Not breathing after head-tilt chin-lift repositioning. Multiple critical injuries from building collapse. 40 other patients are waiting for assessment.<end_of_turn>
<start_of_turn>model
TRIAGE: EXPECTANT (Black)

REASONING — WHO START Protocol:
Step 1 — Breathing: Not breathing after airway repositioning → EXPECTANT.

IMPORTANT: EXPECTANT does not mean this patient cannot survive under normal hospital conditions. It means: with 40 patients waiting and limited resources, diverting resources here costs other survivable patients their lives.

Actions: Comfort measures only. Document time and decision. Reassess immediately if additional responders arrive.

⚠️ EXPECTANT is resource-dependent. Always reassess.<end_of_turn>

<start_of_turn>user
Patient: Female, 29 years old. Breathing 22 per minute. Strong radial pulse. Capillary refill 1.8 seconds. Closed fracture right forearm with visible deformity. Alert, following all commands. Pain 6 out of 10.<end_of_turn>
<start_of_turn>model
TRIAGE: DELAYED (Yellow)

REASONING — WHO START Protocol:
Step 2 — RR: 22/min (10-30) → PASS
Step 3 — Perfusion: Pulse strong, CRT 1.8s < 2s → PASS
Step 4 — Mental Status: Alert, following commands → PASS

All START steps pass → DELAYED.

Closed forearm fracture is serious but not life-threatening. Patient can safely wait 1-2 hours.

Actions: Splint the fracture, elevate, analgesia if available. Reassess every 15-30 minutes.

⚠️ All findings must be verified by trained personnel.<end_of_turn>

<start_of_turn>user
Patient: Male, 60 years old. Crushing chest pain radiating to left arm. Sweating heavily, pale skin. Breathing 24 per minute. Radial pulse present but irregular. SpO2 94 percent. Following commands.<end_of_turn>
<start_of_turn>model
TRIAGE: IMMEDIATE (Red) — CLINICAL OVERRIDE

REASONING:
START Assessment: RR 24 PASS, Pulse present PASS, Following commands PASS → technically DELAYED.

CLINICAL OVERRIDE TO IMMEDIATE:
Crushing chest pain + left arm radiation + diaphoresis + pallor + irregular pulse = acute MI presentation. START was designed for trauma. Medical emergencies require clinical judgment override. This patient will arrest without intervention.

Actions: Aspirin 300mg chewed immediately, high-flow O2, IV access, complete rest, priority cardiac evacuation.

⚠️ Clinical override must be documented with rationale.<end_of_turn>
